source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The sides of the rectangle $A B C D$ are parallel to the co-ordinate axes. The rectangle is positioned below the $x$-axis and to the right of the $y$-axis, as shown in the picture. The co-ordinates of the points $A, B, C, D$ are whole numbers. For each of the points we calculate the value of ( $y$ co-ordinate) $\div$(x co-ordinate). Which of the points will give the smallest value?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly references the diagram with 'as shown in the picture'
the task asks which labeled point A/B/C/D has the smallest y-coordinate divided by x-coordinate, so the point locations/labels must be obtained from the image
multi_step: requires considering all rectangle vertices rather than extracting a single visual fact
solver must use the geometric setup (rectangle aligned to axes, below x-axis, right of y-axis) together with the plotted integer coordinates
determining the smallest value of y/x requires comparing ratios across points, combining both x and y effects
verifiability: answer_type is option/choice with a fixed finite choice set
once the vertex coordinates are read from the diagram, each y/x value can be computed and compared deterministically

decision_reasons: assets are complete: question, answer, and primary image are all present
registry passed with no recorded asset issues
high verifiability and strong image-grounded solvability are supported by the scoring record
although label mapping is not in text, the required diagram is available, so the core semantics are recoverable
